steps.title: Identify the exclusive devotion
steps.content: The passage opens with “Thornton alone held [Buck]” and immediately states “The rest of mankind was as nothing”—establishing that Buck's allegiance belongs to Thornton uniquely.

steps.title: Match to the main idea
steps.content:
D states that Buck holds Thornton in higher regard than any other person, which directly expresses the passage's central claim about their unique relationship.
The other three either isolate a secondary behavior without capturing the relationship hierarchy, suggest unexplained change, or contradict the passage's portrayal.
That confirms D is correct.
